items.title: Stocks to buy in 2026 for long term: TVS Motor, Pidilite Industries among 5 stocks that could give 10-50% return
items.description: Brokerages have turned broadly optimistic on select large- and mid-cap names across consumption, auto, industrials, logistics, and agrochemicals, highlighting strong earnings visibility and attractive risk-reward at current levels.We have collated a list of recommendations from top brokerage firms from ETNow and other sources:

items.title: Positive Breakout: These 13 stocks cross above their 200 DMAs
items.description: In the Nifty500 pack, 13 stocks' closing prices crossed above their 200 DMA (Daily Moving Averages) on February 25, 2026, according to stockedge.com's technical scan data. The 200-day daily moving average (DMA) is used by traders as a key indicator for determining the overall trend in a particular stock. As long as the stock is priced above the 200-day SMA on the daily timeframe, it is generally considered to be in an overall uptrend. Take a look:

items.title: Tariff Tantrum 2.0: Time to look beyond 2026? 6 stocks from different sectors with at least one ‘moat’; and upside potential of up to 31%
items.description: The market can live with clear bullishness or even a bearish trend. What it really hates is uncertainty. And these are uncertain times – marked by a spike in volatility. The absence of a clear trend means that these are uncertain times for investors as well. You could “sit out the volatility”, of course. But if you insist on deploying money in the market, it would be safer to look at stocks that may be from different sectors but have at least one common factor – an edge their peers don’t have.

items.title: Export uncertainty may dull domestic momentum for solar companies
items.description: US imposition of a 125.87% countervailing duty on Indian solar modules is set to impact domestic companies by potentially lowering module prices. Exporters may redirect unsold inventory to India, while companies like Waaree Energies are strengthening US manufacturing to mitigate the effects. The duty erodes future revenue visibility for Indian manufacturers targeting the US market.

items.title: Analysts cautious on IRFC OFS, see limited upside
items.description: The government is selling a stake in Indian Railway Finance Corporation. Analysts suggest caution for investors. The offer for sale aims to meet public shareholding norms. The floor price offers limited discount. IRFC has seen loan growth supported by railway capital expenditure. Future gains may depend on market sentiment.

items.title: GIFT Nifty up 50 points; here's the trading setup for today's session
items.description: Equities advanced on Wednesday amid positive global cues, though trade uncertainties kept sentiment cautious. Analysts anticipate a range-bound market, guided by macro developments and sector-specific catalysts, with immediate support at 25300 and resistance at 25650-25750. Foreign investors showed strong buying interest.

items.title: Stocks in news: IRFC, Adani Ports, Lodha Developers, Lupin, RIL
items.description: Markets closed higher despite initial volatility on the first day of the new expiry series, though analysts suggest potential for further downside. Several companies like IRFC, Adani Ports, Lodha Developers, Lupin, and RIL are in focus due to significant news developments impacting their operations and financial standing.

items.title: Vedanta board clears Rs 3,000 crore NCD fundraise
items.description: Vedanta's board has greenlit a ₹3,000 crore fundraising initiative via unsecured, rated, and listed non-convertible debentures. These instruments, with a face value of ₹1,00,000 each, are slated for listing on the BSE. CRISIL has assigned a 'CRISIL AA' rating to these NCDs, placing them under 'Rating Watch with Developing Implications'.

items.title: Bulk Deal Buzz: Promoters offload 5.3% in Aditya Infotech, shares rally 7.3%
items.description: Promoter Hari Khemka Business Family Trust and Dixon Technologies divested shares of Aditya Infotech (CPPLUS) worth ₹920.8 crore in bulk deals. The entities sold 62.6 lakh shares at ₹1,470 each, offloading 5.3% of the company's total equity. Major mutual funds and HDFC Life Insurance were among the buyers.

items.title: India's stock market offers scale and growth: Pandey
items.description: India is building a transparent, well-governed, and investable stock market to attract long-term institutional capital, according to Sebi chief Tuhin Kanta Pandey. He highlighted that the country offers scale and growth, with foreign portfolio investor assets tripling to ₹71 lakh crore. Domestic institutional investors are now providing a crucial counterbalance, enhancing market resilience.

items.title: Sebi ramps up broker dealing room scrutiny
items.description: India's market regulator is intensifying its watch on stockbrokers' dealing rooms. This move aims to curb front running, a practice where employees exploit insider knowledge of large trades for personal gain. Authorities are investigating how trades are initiated and executed, focusing on institutional and high-net-worth clients.

items.title: SAT adjourns hearing in Jane Street case to April 17
items.description: The Securities Appellate Tribunal has postponed a key hearing. This case involves US trader Jane Street and market regulator Sebi. Sebi had previously imposed a hefty fine and trading ban on Jane Street. The firm has challenged Sebi's order. The tribunal will now hear the matter on April 17. Jane Street had deposited the fine amount.

items.title: Metal stocks rise 7%, analysts see potential upside of up to 25%
items.description: Metal stocks surged over 7% on Wednesday, driven by firm base metal prices and reduced uncertainty over US tariffs. Analysts suggest buying on dips, anticipating up to 25% upside.  Companies like Lloyds Metals, National Aluminium, and Vedanta saw significant gains as investors shifted funds from IT to the buoyant metal sector.

items.title: IT bounce breaks 5-day losing run, but analysts warn relief may be short-lived
items.description: Indian IT stocks saw a brief rebound on Wednesday, ending a losing streak. However, analysts caution this relief might be temporary. Most derivative bets remain bearish after the IT index's substantial drop. The sector experienced pressure throughout February, with new AI tools intensifying concerns about future revenue. Short covering fueled the bounce, but underlying weakness persists.

items.title: RBI summons Yes Bank executives over forex card breach
items.description: The Reserve Bank of India has called in senior Yes Bank executives after a major security incident involving the Yes Bank–BookMyForex multi-currency forex card. According to reports, card numbers and CVV details of numerous customers may have been exposed. The RBI has demanded a full account of how the breach occurred and what safeguards failed.
